description: Add branch-per-execution on plan accept and resume interrupted execution from prd.json

userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/main.rs, add a `--resume` boolean flag to the Cli struct using clap (`#[arg(long)]`). In crates/baro-tui/src/app.rs, add a `resume_mode: bool` field to App (default false) and a `is_resume: bool` field. No other changes needed yet. Ensure `cargo build` passes with zero warnings.
userStories.title: Add --resume CLI flag and resume_mode to App

userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/git.rs, add a public async function `create_or_checkout_branch(cwd: &Path, branch_name: &str) -> Result<(), String>` that: 1) Runs `git checkout -b <branch_name>`. 2) If that fails (branch exists), runs `git checkout <branch_name>`. 3) After successful checkout, attempts `git push -u origin <branch_name>` (best-effort, log but don't fail if no remote). Returns Ok(()) on success or Err with message. Ensure cargo build passes with zero warnings.
userStories.title: Add git branch create/checkout helpers

userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/main.rs, in the Screen::Review Enter key handler, after writing prd.json and before calling spawn_executor, call git::create_or_checkout_branch with the branch name formatted as `baro/<branchName>` from app.branch_name. Make this an async operation - you'll need to restructure slightly: use tokio::spawn to run the branch creation, then start execution. Alternatively, since we're already in an async context via the event loop, spawn a task that creates the branch then starts the executor. In crates/baro-tui/src/screens/execute.rs (or execute_dashboard.rs), update the header/title bar to display the current branch name from app.branch_name. The execute screen header should show something like 'BARO | project-name | baro/branch-name'. Store the full branch path (baro/<name>) in app. Ensure cargo build passes with zero warnings.
userStories.title: Create branch on plan accept and show in TUI header

userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/screens/review.rs, modify the render function to handle resume mode (when app.is_resume is true). In resume mode: 1) Change header title to 'Resume Review' instead of 'Plan Review'. 2) For each story, show a checkmark icon (✓) and green color if the story was completed (you'll need to track this - add a `completed: bool` field to ReviewStory in app.rs). 3) Show incomplete stories with a pending icon (○). 4) Show counts: 'X done, Y remaining'. 5) Footer should say 'Enter:resume' instead of 'Enter:accept'. Ensure cargo build passes with zero warnings.
userStories.title: Create resume review screen variant

userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/main.rs run_app function, add resume detection logic that runs BEFORE the existing goal check. The logic: 1) Check if prd.json exists in cwd. 2) If it exists, read and parse it as PrdFile. 3) Check if any stories have passes=false. 4) If --resume flag is set OR (prd.json exists with incomplete stories AND no goal was provided on CLI), enter resume mode. 5) In resume mode: set app.is_resume = true, populate app.project/branch_name/description from the prd, build ReviewStory list from ALL stories (with completed field set based on passes), call app.show_review() to go directly to the review screen (skipping welcome and planning). 6) In the Review screen Enter handler, when app.is_resume is true, read the existing prd.json instead of building a new one from review_stories (since it has the full data with acceptance/tests). Also checkout the baro/<branchName> branch before starting execution. 7) The DAG engine already filters incomplete stories, so execution will only run remaining ones. Ensure cargo build passes with zero warnings.
userStories.title: Wire resume flow with auto-detection and --resume flag

userStories.description: Run `cargo build` in the project root and fix ANY warnings or errors. Check for: unused imports, unused variables, dead code warnings, missing fields in struct initialization, type mismatches. Fix all issues found. Run `cargo build` again to confirm zero warnings and zero errors. Also verify `cargo clippy` passes cleanly if clippy is available.
userStories.title: Final build verification and warning cleanup
